text: The surgeon carefully dissects the posterior plane during the procedure, revealing the suspect point of infiltration in the mesorectal fascia.
The surgeon deepens the dissection into the presacral fascia to achieve a clear circumferential resection margin, ensuring no involvement of the mesorectal fascia.
The surgeon performs a careful and accurate deeper dissection in the presacral area, aiming to minimize the risk of nerve damage while resecting only the involved tissue.
The surgeon completes the dissection by reaching the end of the mesorectal fascia involvement, re-establishing the clear posterior mesorectal plane to finalize the procedure.
